title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on Who Can Purchase Group Term Life Insurance? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group term life insurance, group term insurance, group term insurance plan
Body Content:
The provision of group term life insurance is made available to a group of people as a whole unit. For instance, if a certain employer wishes to buy insurance for his staff of one department in the company, he can offer employees a group term life insurance policy instead of individual policies for each person. This works out more cost-effective for employers as they have one premium which is considered with a discount depending on how large the group is. Typically, employers offer this to candidates joining an organisation in order to attract talent and maintain employees once they are employed.
Most employers provide group term insurance at no cost to employees. A base amount is offered, with room for employees to top it up for more cover. Employees also see this as a benefit from the company as it gives them and their families some degree of financial security in the future. Should something unfortunate happen to the employee, the group term life insurance policy kicks in and a death benefit is received by the employee’s family.
Who Can Purchase Group Term Life Insurance?
Group term life insurance can be bought for any group. The following kinds of groups are permitted to buy group life insurance:
Consumer Groups
Associations can buy group insurance for members of the association
Employer - Employee
Any Non-profit Organisations/Groups
Any other self-interest groups that are established for a purpose which is other than availing for group insurance
An Employer can Purchase a Group Term Life Insurance Policy for Employee Groups
When an employer buys a group term insurance plan, there are specific conditions that are applicable:
Group insurance can be offered to employees who are classified as being permanent, full-time, or active.
The age of the employee must be 18 or older, or 70 years or below.
The employee should be performing their regular work duties to be classified as “active”. Any employee who is on a leave of absence for the long term due to reasons involving health that the organisation does not recognise, is not considered eligible to get group insurance.
Any employee who is absent from duties as a result of injury, sickness, or maternity leave may be considered as active employees. When registering for group term life insurance, such employees must submit a declaration of health.
A Non-Employer-Employee Group can be Insured, Subject to Conditions Imposed by IRDA
A group that falls under the category of “non-employer-employee” could be members that hold a single identical bank account, members of the same social organisation, or a group of individuals who all belong to the same profession, such as a group of lawyers, journalists, chefs, etc.
Nonetheless, the condition that insurers stipulate for such groups is that the policy must be incidental, and not for the singular purpose of owning insurance. For such groups, the protocol remains identical to any group insurance bought by an employer in a company. Therefore, a sole policy can be issued by insurers to offer cover for different members who have a commonality that make them part of the group. There will be just one master insurance policy. Consequently, eligibility criteria involve members of the group proving their appropriate relationship to the group. For instance, if a group of lawyers is obtaining group term insurance, then each member must be a lawyer to be eligible.
Case on Eligibility of Buying Group Term Cover
The following sample case will illustrate the eligibility for group term cover:
Mobilepart is a supply company for automobile components. The firm has registered offices in different parts of India, namely in Mumbai, Delhi, and Pune. The company is also responsible for the delivery of motorbike parts to dealers, and car components to car dealers. Dealers of commercial vehicles are also on Mobilepart’s list of clients. The company delivers parts to neighbouring cities as well.
Mobilepart has a base of active employees, including office managers, on-site managers, sales staff, and employees in its Delhi head office. The company’s twin offices in Mumbai and Pune are crucial to the growth of the company. In view of this, the head of the Human Resource Department (Mr. Somnath) decides to offer the complete workforce of Mobilepart a sole group term life insurance plan. Mr. Somnath is only able to include permanent staff in this plan. There are certain temporary staff such as labourers and drivers, but they are not eligible to be a part of this insurance plan. However, Mr. Somnath can provide all permanent employees with a loyalty bonus, as well as offer the head office staff a separate group policy based on their seniority.
Conclusion
If you are salaried and about to be offered a group term life insurance policy from an employer, you should not avoid it. If it is part of your benefit scheme, you may have to mandatorily opt for it. In addition to this, it's a good plan to have your own health insurance and life insurance plan as well.
full_pdf_text:
Who Can Purchase Group Term Life Insurance?
The provision of group term life insurance is made available to a group of people as a whole unit. For instance, if a certain employer wishes to buy insurance for his staff of one department in the company, he can offer employees a group term life insurance policy instead of individual policies for each person. This works out more cost-effective for employers as they have to pay premium which is considered depending on how large the group is. Typically, employers offer this to candidates joining an organisation as one of the ways to attract talent and retain the employees once they are employed. Most employers provide group term insurance at no cost to employees. A base sum assured is offered, with room for employees to top it up for more cover as per terms & conditions stated in the group policy. Employees also see this as a benefit from the company as it gives them and their families some degree of financial security in the future. Should something unfortunate happens to the employee, the group term life insurance policy kicks in and a death benefit is received by the employee’s nominee/s basis policy terms & conditions. Who Can Purchase Group Term Life Insurance? Group term life insurance can be bought for any group. The following kinds of groups are few of the examples who can buy group life insurance1:
● Consumer Groups ● Associations can buy group insurance for members of the association ● Employer - Employee ● Any Non-profit Organisations/Groups ● Any other self-interest groups that are established for a purpose which is other than availing for group insurance An Employer Can Purchase a Group Term Life Insurance Policy for Employee Groups When an employer buys a group term insurance plan, there are specific conditions that are applicable depending upon the terms and condition offered under the Group Plan of the insurer.
● Group insurance can be offered to employees who are classified as being permanent, full- time, or active or on probation. ● The age of the employee must be 18 or older, or 70 years or below. ● Max age at Maturity is 79 for Employer-Employee and 69 for Non Employer-Employee. ● Declaration of Good Health is required in Employer-Employee only in case of break in service, or additional Top Up cover A Non-Employer-Employee Group A group that falls under the category of “non-employer-employee” could be members that that belong to a particular group like account holders of a particular bank, members of the same social
organisation, or a group of individuals who all belong to the same profession, such as a group of lawyers, journalists, chefs, etc. Nonetheless, the condition that insurers stipulate for such groups is that the policy must be incidental, and the group must not be formed for the singular purpose of owning insurance. Therefore, a single policy is issued by insurers to offer cover for multiple members who have a commonality that make them part of the group. This is called Master Policy. The Certificate of Insurance is issued to the members, and premiums are paid by the members, and facilitated through the Master Policyholder. Consequently, eligibility criteria involve members of the group proving their appropriate relationship to the group. Case on Eligibility of Buying Group Term Cover The following sample case will illustrate the eligibility for group term cover: Mobilepart is a supply company for automobile components. The firm has registered offices in different parts of India, namely in Mumbai, Delhi, and Pune. The company is also responsible for the delivery of motorbike parts to dealers, and car components to car dealers. Dealers of commercial vehicles are also on Mobilepart’s list of clients. The company delivers parts to neighbouring cities as well. Mobilepart has a base of active employees, including office managers, on-site managers, sales staff, and employees in its Delhi head office. The company’s twin offices in Mumbai and Pune are crucial to the growth of the company. In view of this, the head of the Human Resource Department (Mr. Somnath) decides to offer the complete workforce of Mobilepart a single group term life insurance Policy. Mr. Somnath is only able to include permanent staff in this plan. There are certain temporary staff such as labourers and drivers, but they are not eligible to be a part of this insurance plan. However, Mr. Somnath can offer the head office staff a separate group policy based on their seniority. Conclusion If you are salaried and about to be offered a group term life insurance policy from an employer, you should consider, not avoiding it. If it is part of your benefit scheme, you may have to mandatorily opt for it. In addition to this, it's preferable to have your own life insurance plan as well. Source:
1 https://securenow.in/insuropedia/who-can-purchase-group-term-life-insurance/ Disclaimer: The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
